name: empty_vecs_always_equal
code:
proof fn phi_1_empty_vecs_always_equal(e1: Vec<u8>, e2: Vec<u8>)
    requires
        e1@.len() == 0,
        e2@.len() == 0,
    ensures
        e1@ == e2@,
{
}
reason: Two empty Vec<u8> should be equal at the spec level — if this fails it would indicate broken extensional equality for empty sequences

name: match_result_ignores_capacity
code:
proof fn phi_2_match_result_ignores_capacity(e1: Vec<u8>, e2: Vec<u8>, e3: Vec<u8>)
    requires
        e1@ == e2@,
        e2@ == e3@,
    ensures
        e1@ == e3@,
{
}
reason: Transitivity of sequence equality must hold — if the spec-level view equality is not transitive, chained comparisons via `do_vec_u8s_match` would be unsound

name: single_element_diff_detected
code:
proof fn phi_3_single_element_diff_detected(e1: Vec<u8>, e2: Vec<u8>, i: int)
    requires
        e1@.len() == e2@.len(),
        0 <= i < e1@.len(),
        e1@[i] != e2@[i],
    ensures
        e1@ != e2@,
{
}
reason: If two sequences differ at any index, they must be unequal — if extensional equality somehow missed a single-element difference, the function's ensures would be violated

name: length_mismatch_implies_unequal
code:
proof fn phi_4_length_mismatch_implies_unequal(e1: Vec<u8>, e2: Vec<u8>)
    requires
        e1@.len() != e2@.len(),
    ensures
        e1@ != e2@,
{
}
reason: Sequences of different lengths must be unequal — if the spec allowed length-mismatched sequences to be equal, the early return in `do_vec_u8s_match` would be wrong

name: pointwise_equal_implies_seq_equal
reason: The function relies on pointwise comparison implying full sequence equality via extensional equality — if this entailment broke, the function's loop-based comparison would not establish its postcondition
